mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals resilient consumer spending that lifts corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes are followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observed months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held below 0.25% suppresses short-term discount rates and reduces the opportunity cost of holding equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns during triggered periods are negative on average or the Sharpe ratio falls below zero over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a broad-based labor market recovery that boosts consumption forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The joint movement is required because either alone reflects compositional shifts rather than genuine demand strength.
failure_condition: The interaction term of PAYEMS and UNRATE changes fails to significantly improve prediction of S&P 500 20-day returns beyond individual series in a nested regression with F-test p-value greater than 0.05 over any 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns when inflation is low and stable, as rising output expands corporate revenues without triggering offsetting margin compression from input costs.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth coincides with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 45% of low-inflation observations during a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income and weakening aggregate demand, causing investors to revise earnings forecasts downward and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by at least 0.1pp from prior month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises by 0.1 percentage point or more during a 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts signal durable construction activity and backward linkages to materials and employment, lifting cyclical earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST surprises are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of non-recessionary quarters over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero while consumer prices rise, negative real interest rates force investors out the risk curve into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This requires both the nominal rate and inflation level to be observed jointly.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25 and CPIAUCSL year-over-year change above 1.5
regime_hint: zero lower bound with positive inflation
failure_condition: The interaction of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to add explanatory power with incremental R-squared below 0.01 for S&P 500 20-day returns in a walk-forward regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth confirms the economy is expanding above stall speed, reducing recession probability and supporting a risk-on reallocation that lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth above 1 percent
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of quarters when GDPC1 growth exceeds 1% annualized during a 5-year walk-forward test.
